400     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for GPG key parsing");
401     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
402   }
403 
404   *num_keys = 0;
405   *keys_out = NULL;
406 
407   // Check if this looks like a GPG key (starts with -----BEGIN PGP)
408   if (strncmp(response_text, "-----BEGIN PGP", 14) != 0) {
409     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Response does not contain a valid GPG key");
410     return ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY;
411   }
412 
413   // Write armored block to temp file
414   char temp_file[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
415   int fd = -1;
416   if (platform_create_temp_file(temp_file, sizeof(temp_file), "asc_gpg_import", &fd) != 0) {
417     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to create temp file for GPG import");
418   }
419 
420 #ifdef _WIN32
421   // On Windows, platform_create_temp_file returns fd=-1, need to open separately
422   fd = platform_open("https_key_temp", temp_file, O_WRONLY | O_BINARY);
423   if (fd < 0) {
424     platform_delete_temp_file(temp_file);
425     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to open temp file for writing");
426   }
427 #endif
428 
429   ssize_t written = write(fd, response_text, response_len);
430   close(fd);
431 
432   if (written != (ssize_t)response_len) {
433     platform_unlink(temp_file);
434     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to write GPG key to temp file");
435   }
436 
437   // Import the key using gpg --import
438   char import_cmd[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
439   safe_snprintf(import_cmd, sizeof(import_cmd), "gpg --import '%s' 2>&1", temp_file);
440   FILE *import_fp = NULL;
441   if (platform_popen("gpg_https_import", import_cmd, "r", &import_fp) != ASCIICHAT_OK || !import_fp) {
442     platform_unlink(temp_file);
443     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to run gpg --import");
444   }
445 
446   char import_output[2048];
447   size_t import_len = fread(import_output, 1, sizeof(import_output) - 1, import_fp);
448   import_output[import_len] = '\0';
449   platform_pclose(&import_fp);
450   platform_unlink(temp_file);
451 
452   // Extract ALL key IDs from import output (format: "gpg: key KEYID: ...")
453   // GitHub often returns multiple keys in one armored block
454   const char *key_marker = "gpg: key ";
455   char key_ids[16][17]; // Support up to 16 keys
456   size_t key_count = 0;
457 
458   log_debug("GPG import output:\n%s", import_output);
459 
460   char *search_pos = import_output;
461   while (key_count < 16) {
462     char *key_line = strstr(search_pos, key_marker);
463     if (!key_line)
464       break;
465 
466     key_line += strlen(key_marker);
467     int i = 0;
468     while (i < 16 && key_line[i] != ':' && key_line[i] != ' ' && key_line[i] != '\n') {
469       key_ids[key_count][i] = key_line[i];
470       i++;
471     }
472     key_ids[key_count][i] = '\0';
473 
474     if (i > 0) {
475       log_debug("Extracted GPG key ID #%zu: %s", key_count, key_ids[key_count]);
476       key_count++;
477     }
478     search_pos = key_line + i;
479   }
480 
481   if (key_count == 0) {
482     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to extract any key IDs from GPG import output");
483   }
484 
485   log_debug("Total GPG keys extracted from import: %zu", key_count);
486 
487   // Allocate array for results
488   *keys_out = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(char *) * key_count, char **);
489   if (!*keys_out) {
490     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate GPG keys array");
491   }
492 
493   // Process each key ID to get full fingerprint
494   size_t valid_keys = 0;
495   for (size_t k = 0; k < key_count; k++) {
496     // Get full fingerprint from gpg --list-keys output
497     char list_cmd[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
498     safe_snprintf(list_cmd, sizeof(list_cmd),
499                   "gpg --list-keys --with-colons --fingerprint '%s' " PLATFORM_SHELL_NULL_REDIRECT, key_ids[k]);
500     FILE *list_fp = NULL;
501     if (platform_popen("gpg_https_list", list_cmd, "r", &list_fp) != ASCIICHAT_OK || !list_fp) {
502       continue; // Skip this key if we can't list it
503     }
504 
505     char list_output[4096];
506     size_t list_len = fread(list_output, 1, sizeof(list_output) - 1, list_fp);
507     list_output[list_len] = '\0';
508     platform_pclose(&list_fp);
509 
510     // Check if it contains an Ed25519 key (algorithm 22)
511     if (!strstr(list_output, ":22:") && !strstr(list_output, "ed25519")) {
512       continue; // Skip non-Ed25519 keys
513     }
514 
515     // Extract full 40-character fingerprint from "fpr:" line
516     // Format: fpr:::::::::FINGERPRINT:
517     // The fingerprint is field 10 (after 9 colons)
518     char fingerprint[41] = {0};
519     const char *fpr_marker = "\nfpr:";
520     char *fpr_line = strstr(list_output, fpr_marker);
521     if (fpr_line) {
522       fpr_line += 1; // Skip the newline
523       // Count 9 colons from the start of the line
524       int colon_count = 0;
525       while (*fpr_line && colon_count < 9) {
526         if (*fpr_line == ':')
527           colon_count++;
528         fpr_line++;
529       }
530       // Now we should be at the start of the fingerprint
531       // Extract up to 40 hex characters
532       int fpr_len = 0;
533       while (fpr_len < 40 && fpr_line[fpr_len] && fpr_line[fpr_len] != ':' && fpr_line[fpr_len] != '\n') {
534         fingerprint[fpr_len] = fpr_line[fpr_len];
535         fpr_len++;
536       }
537       fingerprint[fpr_len] = '\0';
538     }
539 
540     // If fingerprint extraction failed, use the short key ID
541     if (strlen(fingerprint) == 0) {
542       log_warn("Failed to extract fingerprint for key %s, using short key ID", key_ids[k]);
543       SAFE_STRNCPY(fingerprint, key_ids[k], sizeof(fingerprint));
544     }
545 
546     log_debug("Key %s -> fingerprint: %s (length: %zu)", key_ids[k], fingerprint, strlen(fingerprint));
547 
548     // Allocate and store this key in gpg:KEYID format
549     size_t gpg_key_len = strlen("gpg:") + strlen(fingerprint) + 1;
550     (*keys_out)[valid_keys] = SAFE_MALLOC(gpg_key_len, char *);
551     if (!(*keys_out)[valid_keys]) {
552       // Cleanup on allocation failure
553       for (size_t cleanup = 0; cleanup < valid_keys; cleanup++) {
554         SAFE_FREE((*keys_out)[cleanup]);
555       }
556       SAFE_FREE(*keys_out);
557       *keys_out = NULL;
558       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate GPG key string");
559     }
560 
561     safe_snprintf((*keys_out)[valid_keys], gpg_key_len, "gpg:%s", fingerprint);
562     log_debug("Added valid Ed25519 key #%zu: %s", valid_keys, (*keys_out)[valid_keys]);
563     valid_keys++;
564   }
565 
566   if (valid_keys == 0) {
567     SAFE_FREE(*keys_out);
568     *keys_out = NULL;
569     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "No valid Ed25519 keys found in imported GPG keys");
570   }
571 
572   *num_keys = valid_keys;
573   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
574 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM,
    BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL, ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY, log_debug,
    log_warn, platform_create_temp_file(),
    platform_delete_temp_file(), PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH,
    platform_open(), platform_pclose(), platform_popen(),
    PLATFORM_SHELL_NULL_REDIRECT, platform_unlink(),
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(),
    SAFE_STRNCPY, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by fetch_github_gpg_keys(), and
    fetch_gitlab_gpg_keys().






asciichat_error_t parse_keys_from_file (const char * path,
  public_key_t * keys, size_t * num_keys, size_t max_keys)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/crypto/keys.h>


Parse SSH keys from file (supports authorized_keys and known_hosts
    formats).


Parameters


path Path to key file (must not be NULL)


Key Management Output array for parsed public keys (must
  not be NULL)


num_keys Output parameter for number of keys parsed (must not be NULL)


max_keys Maximum number of keys to parse (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Parses multiple SSH keys from file. Supports:


    
  
  • authorized_keys format: One key per line (ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3...
      comment)
    • 
  
  • .pub file with multiple entries: File containing multiple SSH
      public key entries, one per line (each line in ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3...
      comment format). This format is similar to authorized_keys and can contain
      any number of keys.
    • 
  
  • known_hosts format: Multiple keys per line (hostname ssh-ed25519
      AAAAC3... comment)
    • 



File format examples:




# authorized_keys format or .pub file with multiple entries
ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI... alice@example.com
ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI... bob@example.com



Note


Key filtering: Only Ed25519 and X25519 keys are parsed.
  RSA/ECDSA keys are skipped (not supported).



File format: Automatically detects file format based on content.
    Supports both authorized_keys format (one key per line) and known_hosts
    format. Also supports .pub files containing multiple key entries (one per
    line).




Maximum keys: Stops parsing after max_keys keys are found.
    Function returns ASCIICHAT_OK even if more keys exist in file.




Warning


File must exist and be readable. Returns error if file
  cannot be opened.


Definition at line 544 of file keys.c.


544                                                                                                                 {
545   if (!path || !keys || !num_keys) {
546     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for key file parsing");
547   }
548 
549   *num_keys = 0;
550 
551   if (!path_looks_like_path(path)) {
552     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid keys file path: %s", path);
553   }
554 
555   char *normalized_path = NULL;
556   asciichat_error_t path_result = path_validate_user_path(path, PATH_ROLE_CLIENT_KEYS, &normalized_path);
557   if (path_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
558     SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
559     return path_result;
560   }
561 
562   FILE *f = platform_fopen("file_stream", normalized_path, "r");
563   if (!f) {
564     SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
565     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to open keys file: %s", path);
566   }
567 
568   char line[BUFFER_SIZE_LARGE];
569   while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), f) && *num_keys < max_keys) {
570     // Remove newline
571     line[strcspn(line, "\r\n")] = 0;
572 
573     // Skip empty lines and comments
574     if (strlen(line) == 0 || line[0] == '#') {
575       continue;
576     }
577 
578     if (parse_public_key(line, &keys[*num_keys]) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
579       (*num_keys)++;
580     }
581   }
582 
583   (void)fclose(f);
584   SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
585   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
586 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, BUFFER_SIZE_LARGE,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    parse_public_key(), path_looks_like_path(),
    PATH_ROLE_CLIENT_KEYS, path_validate_user_path(),
    platform_fopen(), SAFE_FREE, and SET_ERRNO.

asciichat_error_t parse_private_key (const char * path,
  private_key_t * key_out)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/crypto/keys.h>


Parse SSH private key from file.


Parameters


path Path to private key file


key_out Output structure for parsed private key (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Parses private key from file. Supports:


    
  
  • OpenSSH Ed25519: ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 (OpenSSH Ed25519 format)
    • 
  
  • Raw hex file: File containing 64 hex chars for X25519 key
    • 



Note


Encrypted keys: If key is encrypted (has password),
  prompts for password and decrypts it using platform-specific password prompt.



SSH agent detection: If use_ssh_agent is true, key is loaded from
    SSH agent instead of file. Key stays in agent (not loaded into memory).




File permissions: Validates file permissions before parsing. Warns
    if file has overly permissive permissions (world-readable).




Key format: Currently only supports Ed25519 keys (OpenSSH format).
    RSA/ECDSA keys are NOT supported.




Warning


File permissions: Private key files should have
  restrictive permissions (0600). Function warns but does not fail on overly
  permissive permissions.


Definition at line 193 of file keys.c.


193                                                                                   {
194   if (!key_path || !key_out) {
195     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for private key parsing");
196   }
197 
198   // Clear output structure
199   memset(key_out, 0, sizeof(private_key_t));
200 
201   // Check for GPG key format: "gpg:KEYID" where KEYID is 8, 16, or 40 hex chars
202   // - 8 chars: short key ID (last 8 chars of fingerprint)
203   // - 16 chars: long key ID (last 16 chars of fingerprint)
204   // - 40 chars: full fingerprint
205   if (strncmp(key_path, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
206     const char *key_id = key_path + 4; // Skip "gpg:" prefix
207 
208     // Validate key ID format (must be 8, 16, or 40 hex characters)
209     size_t key_id_len = strlen(key_id);
210     if (key_id_len != 8 && key_id_len != 16 && key_id_len != 40) {
211       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid GPG key ID length: %zu (expected 8, 16, or 40 hex chars)",
212                        key_id_len);
213     }
214 
215     // Validate hex characters
216     for (size_t i = 0; i < key_id_len; i++) {
217       char c = key_id[i];
218       if (!((c >= '0' && c <= '9') || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F'))) {
219         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid GPG key ID: contains non-hex character '%c'", c);
220       }
221     }
222 
223     // Get public key and keygrip from GPG keyring
224     uint8_t public_key[32];
225     char keygrip[64];
226     if (gpg_get_public_key(key_id, public_key, keygrip) != 0) {
227       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to get public key from GPG for key ID: %s", key_id);
228     }
229 
230     // Populate private_key_t structure for GPG agent use
231     key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
232     key_out->use_gpg_agent = true;
233     key_out->use_ssh_agent = false;
234 
235     // Store public key (for handshake and verification)
236     memcpy(key_out->public_key, public_key, 32);
237 
238     // Store keygrip (for GPG agent signing operations)
239     platform_strncpy(key_out->gpg_keygrip, sizeof(key_out->gpg_keygrip), keygrip, sizeof(key_out->gpg_keygrip) - 1);
240 
241     // Store comment for display
242     safe_snprintf(key_out->key_comment, sizeof(key_out->key_comment), "GPG key %s", key_id);
243 
244     // Clear the key.ed25519 field (we don't have the private key in memory)
245     // The private key stays protected in GPG agent
246     memset(key_out->key.ed25519, 0, 64);
247 
248     // Set the public key in the second half of ed25519 key (standard Ed25519 format)
249     // This is for compatibility with code that expects the public key at offset 32
250     memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519 + 32, public_key, 32);
251 
252     log_debug("Loaded GPG key %s (keygrip: %.40s) for agent signing", key_id, keygrip);
253     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
254   }
255 
256   // Try to load file and detect format
257   char *normalized_path = NULL;
258   asciichat_error_t path_result = path_validate_user_path(key_path, PATH_ROLE_KEY_PRIVATE, &normalized_path);
259   if (path_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
260     SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
261     return path_result;
262   }
263 
264   // Read file content to detect format
265   FILE *f = platform_fopen("file_stream", normalized_path, "r");
266   if (!f) {
267     SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
268     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to open private key file: %s", key_path);
269   }
270 
271   // Read first 50 bytes to detect format
272   char header[50];
273   size_t bytes_read = fread(header, 1, sizeof(header) - 1, f);
274   header[bytes_read] = '\0';
275   fclose(f);
276 
277   log_debug("parse_private_key: Read %zu bytes from %s, header: %.50s", bytes_read, key_path, header);
278 
279   // Check for PGP armored secret key format
280   if (strstr(header, "-----BEGIN PGP PRIVATE KEY") != NULL || strstr(header, "-----BEGIN PGP SECRET KEY") != NULL) {
281     log_debug("Detected PGP armored secret key format in %s", key_path);
282     // Load entire file for OpenPGP parsing
283     f = platform_fopen("file_stream", normalized_path, "r");
284     if (!f) {
285       SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
286       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to open GPG key file: %s", key_path);
287     }
288 
289     // Get file size
290     fseek(f, 0, SEEK_END);
291     long file_size = ftell(f);
292     fseek(f, 0, SEEK_SET);
293 
294     if (file_size <= 0 || file_size > 1024 * 1024) { // Sanity check: max 1MB
295       fclose(f);
296       SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
297       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid GPG key file size: %ld bytes", file_size);
298     }
299 
300     char *file_content = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)file_size + 1, char *);
301     size_t content_read = fread(file_content, 1, (size_t)file_size, f);
302     file_content[content_read] = '\0';
303     fclose(f);
304 
305     // Parse OpenPGP armored secret key
306     uint8_t ed25519_pk[32];
307     uint8_t ed25519_sk[32];
308     asciichat_error_t openpgp_result = openpgp_parse_armored_seckey(file_content, ed25519_pk, ed25519_sk);
309     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
310     SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
311 
312     if (openpgp_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
313       return openpgp_result;
314     }
315 
316     // Populate private_key_t structure
317     key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
318     key_out->use_gpg_agent = false;
319     key_out->use_ssh_agent = false;
320 
321     // Store keypair in Ed25519 format (64 bytes: 32-byte seed + 32-byte public key)
322     // libsodium expects the seed in first 32 bytes and public key in second 32 bytes
323     memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519, ed25519_sk, 32);      // Secret key (seed)
324     memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519 + 32, ed25519_pk, 32); // Public key
325     memcpy(key_out->public_key, ed25519_pk, 32);       // Also store in public_key field
326 
327     // Store comment
328     safe_snprintf(key_out->key_comment, sizeof(key_out->key_comment), "GPG Ed25519 key from %s", key_path);
329 
330     log_debug("Loaded unencrypted GPG Ed25519 key from %s", key_path);
331     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
332   }
333 
334   // Fall back to SSH private key parsing
335   asciichat_error_t result = parse_ssh_private_key(normalized_path, key_out);
336   SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
337   return result;
338 }

    ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    gpg_get_public_key(), private_key_t::gpg_keygrip,

    KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    openpgp_parse_armored_seckey(), parse_ssh_private_key(),

    platform_fopen(), platform_strncpy(),
    private_key_t::public_key, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,

asciichat_error_t parse_public_key (const char * input,
  public_key_t * key_out)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/crypto/keys.h>


Parse SSH/GPG public key from any format (returns first key
  only).


Parameters


input Key input in various formats (see below)


key_out Output structure for parsed public key (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Parses public key from various input formats:




Supported formats:


    
  
  • Raw base64 SSH key: "AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI..." (just the
      base64 part from .pub files, no prefix)
    • 
  
  • SSH Ed25519: "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3... comment" (full SSH public
      key format)
    • 
  
  • HTTPS URL: "https://example.com/key.pub" or
      "https://example.com/key.gpg" (fetches from URL, parses
      response)
    • 
  
  • GitHub SSH: "github:username" (fetches from
      https://github.com/username.keys, returns first Ed25519 key)
    • 
  
  • GitLab SSH: "gitlab:username" (fetches from
      https://gitlab.com/username.keys, returns first Ed25519 key)
    • 
  
  • GitHub GPG: "github:username.gpg" (fetches GPG key from
      https://github.com/username.gpg)
    • 
  
  • GitLab GPG: "gitlab:username.gpg" (fetches GPG key from
      https://gitlab.com/username.gpg)
    • 
  
  • GPG keyring: "gpg:0xKEYID" (shells out to gpg --export
    KEYID)
    • 
  
  • File path: Path to .pub file or any file containing key (reads first
    line)
    • 
  
  • Raw hex: 64 hex chars for X25519 key
    • 



File support: When a file path is provided, the file is
    read and the first line is parsed as an SSH public key. Common formats:


    
  
  • .pub file: Standard SSH public key file (one key per file, reads first
      line)
    • 
  
  • Any text file: First line containing SSH key format is used
    • 



Note


Key normalization: All keys are converted to X25519
  format for key exchange. Ed25519 keys are converted using libsodium conversion
  function.



GPG support: GPG Ed25519 keys are fully supported. Accepts 8, 16,
    or 40 character key IDs (short/long/full fingerprint). Requires gpg binary
    in PATH and gpg-agent running.




HTTPS URLs: Can be any HTTPS URL (e.g.,
    https://discovery.ascii-chat.com/key.pub). Response content is parsed as key
    format. Uses BearSSL with system CA certificates. Requires network
    connectivity. Only first key is returned; for multiple keys, store them one
    per line in the response and use parse_public_keys()
  instead.




GitHub/GitLab fetching: Uses BearSSL for HTTPS requests. Requires
    network connectivity. Only first Ed25519 key is returned. For multiple keys,
    use parse_public_keys() instead.




File path: Reads first line of file and parses as SSH key format.
    File must exist and be readable. For files with multiple keys (one per
    line), use parse_keys_from_file() instead.




Warning


Network operations: GitHub/GitLab fetching requires
  network connectivity. May fail if network is unavailable or endpoints are
  blocked.


Definition at line 28 of file keys.c.


28                                                                              {
29   if (!input || !key_out) {
30     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for public key parsing");
31   }
32 
33   // Clear output structure
34   memset(key_out, 0, sizeof(public_key_t));
35 
36   // Try SSH key parsing first (full format with prefix)
37   if (strncmp(input, "ssh-ed25519", 11) == 0) {
38     key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
39     asciichat_error_t result = parse_ssh_ed25519_line(input, key_out->key);
40     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
41       platform_strncpy(key_out->comment, sizeof(key_out->comment), "ssh-ed25519", sizeof(key_out->comment) - 1);
42     }
43     return result;
44   }
45 
46   // Try raw base64 SSH public key (without "ssh-ed25519" prefix)
47   // SSH Ed25519 public keys in base64 are typically ~68 characters
48   size_t input_len = strlen(input);
49   if (input_len >= 50 && input_len <= 80) {
50     // Check if it looks like base64 (only contains valid base64 chars)
51     bool looks_like_base64 = true;
52     for (size_t i = 0; i < input_len; i++) {
53       char c = input[i];
54       if (!((c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') || (c >= '0' && c <= '9') || c == '+' || c == '/' ||
55             c == '=')) {
56         looks_like_base64 = false;
57         break;
58       }
59     }
60 
61     if (looks_like_base64) {
62       // Try to decode as SSH public key blob
63       uint8_t *blob = SAFE_MALLOC(input_len, uint8_t *);
64       size_t blob_len = 0;
65 
66       const char *end = NULL;
67       int decode_result = sodium_base642bin(blob, input_len, input, input_len,
68                                             NULL, // ignore chars
69                                             &blob_len, &end, sodium_base64_VARIANT_ORIGINAL);
70 
71       if (decode_result == 0 && blob_len >= 47) { // Minimum size: 4+11+4+32 = 51 bytes
72         // Validate SSH key blob structure
73         // Format: [4 bytes: length][11 bytes: "ssh-ed25519"][4 bytes: length][32 bytes: key]
74         if (blob_len >= 4) {
75           uint32_t key_type_len =
76               ((uint32_t)blob[0] << 24) | ((uint32_t)blob[1] << 16) | ((uint32_t)blob[2] << 8) | blob[3];
77           if (key_type_len == 11 && blob_len >= 15 && memcmp(blob + 4, "ssh-ed25519", 11) == 0) {
78             // Valid ssh-ed25519 blob, extract the 32-byte public key
79             if (blob_len >= 19) {
80               uint32_t pubkey_len =
81                   ((uint32_t)blob[15] << 24) | ((uint32_t)blob[16] << 16) | ((uint32_t)blob[17] << 8) | blob[18];
82               if (pubkey_len == 32 && blob_len >= 51) {
83                 // Extract Ed25519 public key
84                 key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
85                 memcpy(key_out->key, blob + 19, 32);
86                 platform_strncpy(key_out->comment, sizeof(key_out->comment), "raw-base64",
87                                  sizeof(key_out->comment) - 1);
88                 SAFE_FREE(blob);
89                 return ASCIICHAT_OK;
90               }
91             }
92           }
93         }
94       }
95       SAFE_FREE(blob);
96     }
97   }
98 
99   // Try GPG key parsing
100   if (strncmp(input, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
101     return parse_gpg_key(input + 4, key_out);
102   }
103 
104   // Try HTTPS URLs (validated via production-grade URL regex)
105   if (url_is_valid(input)) {
106     // Parse HTTPS URL to extract hostname and path
107     url_parts_t url_parts = {0};
108     asciichat_error_t parse_result = url_parse(input, &url_parts);
109     if (parse_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
110       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to parse HTTPS URL: %s", input);
111     }
112 
113     // Fetch the key via HTTPS
114     char *response = https_get(url_parts.host, url_parts.path);
115     url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
116 
117     if (!response) {
118       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to fetch key from HTTPS URL: %s", input);
119     }
120 
121     // Parse the fetched content as a public key
122     asciichat_error_t result = parse_public_key(response, key_out);
123     SAFE_FREE(response);
124     return result;
125   }
126 
127   // Try HTTPS key fetching (GitHub/GitLab) - delegate to parse_public_keys and return first
128   if (strncmp(input, "github:", 7) == 0 || strncmp(input, "gitlab:", 7) == 0) {
129     public_key_t keys[1];
130     size_t num_keys = 0;
131     asciichat_error_t result = parse_public_keys(input, keys, &num_keys, 1);
132     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK && num_keys > 0) {
133       *key_out = keys[0];
134     }
135     return result;
136   }
137 
138   // Try raw hex key (64 hex chars = 32 bytes)
139   if (strlen(input) == 64) {
140     // Check if it's valid hex
141     bool is_valid_hex = true;
142     for (int i = 0; i < 64; i++) {
143       if (!((input[i] >= '0' && input[i] <= '9') || (input[i] >= 'a' && input[i] <= 'f') ||
144             (input[i] >= 'A' && input[i] <= 'F'))) {
145         is_valid_hex = false;
146         break;
147       }
148     }
149 
150     if (is_valid_hex) {
151       // Assume it's a raw X25519 public key in hex (default for raw hex)
152       key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_X25519;
153       // Use hex_decode utility to safely decode hex string to binary
154       if (hex_decode(input, key_out->key, 32) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
155         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to decode hex key");
156       }
157       platform_strncpy(key_out->comment, sizeof(key_out->comment), "raw-hex", sizeof(key_out->comment) - 1);
158       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
159     }
160   }
161 
162   // Try PGP armored format (-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----)
163   if (strstr(input, "-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----") != NULL) {
164     return parse_gpg_key_binary((const uint8_t *)input, strlen(input), key_out);
165   }
166 
167   if (path_looks_like_path(input)) {
168     char *normalized_path = NULL;
169     asciichat_error_t path_result = path_validate_user_path(input, PATH_ROLE_KEY_PUBLIC, &normalized_path);
170     if (path_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
171       SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
172       return path_result;
173     }
174 
175     FILE *f = platform_fopen("file_stream", normalized_path, "r");
176     if (f) {
177       char line[BUFFER_SIZE_LARGE];
178       if (fgets(line, sizeof(line), f)) {
179         (void)fclose(f);
180         SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
181         // Remove newline
182         line[strcspn(line, "\r\n")] = 0;
183         return parse_public_key(line, key_out);
184       }
185       (void)fclose(f);
186     }
187     SAFE_FREE(normalized_path);
188   }
189 
190   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Unsupported key format: %s", input);
191 }

asciichat_error_t parse_public_keys (const char * input,
  public_key_t * keys_out, size_t * num_keys, size_t max_keys)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/crypto/keys.h>


Parse all SSH/GPG public keys from any format (returns all
  keys).


Parameters


input Key input in various formats (see
  parse_public_key for supported formats)


keys_out Output array for parsed public keys (must not be NULL)


num_keys Output parameter for number of keys parsed (must not be NULL)


max_keys Maximum number of keys to parse (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Similar to parse_public_key(), but returns ALL keys for
    formats that support multiple keys:




Multiple key support:


    
  
  • GitHub SSH: "github:username" - returns ALL Ed25519 keys from
      user's profile
    • 
  
  • GitLab SSH: "gitlab:username" - returns ALL Ed25519 keys from
      user's profile
    • 
  
  • File path: Returns all keys from file (one per line)
    • 
  
  • Comma-separated list: "github:user1, /path/to/keys.pub,
      https://example.com/key.pub" - returns all keys from all sources
    • 



Single key formats (behaves like parse_public_key):


    
  
  • SSH Ed25519: "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3..." - returns single key
    • 
  
  • Raw hex: 64 hex chars - returns single key
    • 
  
  • GPG formats: Returns single key
    • 



Comma-separated support: When input contains commas, each
    comma-separated specifier is parsed independently. Whitespace around commas
    is automatically trimmed. If any specifier fails to parse, that specifier is
    skipped with a warning, but parsing continues for remaining specifiers.
    Returns ASCIICHAT_OK if at least one specifier parsed successfully.




This function is useful when you need to verify against ANY of a
    user's keys, such as when a user has multiple SSH keys for different
    machines on their GitHub/GitLab account, or keys from multiple sources.




Note


GitHub/GitLab: Users often have multiple SSH keys for
  different machines. This function fetches all keys so you can verify against
  any of them.



Key filtering: Only Ed25519 keys are returned. RSA/ECDSA keys are
    skipped.




Warning


Network operations: GitHub/GitLab fetching requires
  network connectivity.


Definition at line 344 of file keys.c.


344                                                                                                                   {
345   if (!input || !keys_out || !num_keys || max_keys == 0) {
346     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for multi-key parsing");
347   }
348 
349   *num_keys = 0;
350 
351   // Handle comma-separated key specifiers
352   if (strchr(input, ',') != NULL) {
353     size_t input_len = strlen(input);
354     char *input_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(input_len + 1, char *);
355     memcpy(input_copy, input, input_len + 1);
356 
357     char *saveptr = NULL;
358     char *specifier = platform_strtok_r(input_copy, ",", &saveptr);
359 
360     while (specifier != NULL && *num_keys < max_keys) {
361       // Trim leading/trailing whitespace
362       while (*specifier && isspace((unsigned char)*specifier)) {
363         specifier++;
364       }
365       char *end = specifier + strlen(specifier) - 1;
366       while (end > specifier && isspace((unsigned char)*end)) {
367         *end-- = '\0';
368       }
369 
370       // Skip empty specifiers
371       if (strlen(specifier) > 0) {
372         // Recursively parse this single specifier (will not re-enter comma handling)
373         size_t current_num = 0;
374         asciichat_error_t result =
375             parse_public_keys(specifier, &keys_out[*num_keys], &current_num, max_keys - *num_keys);
376         if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK && current_num > 0) {
377           *num_keys += current_num;
378         } else {
379           log_warn("Failed to parse key specifier: %s", specifier);
380           // Continue parsing remaining specifiers even if one fails
381         }
382       }
383 
384       specifier = platform_strtok_r(NULL, ",", &saveptr);
385     }
386 
387     SAFE_FREE(input_copy);
388 
389     if (*num_keys == 0) {
390       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "No valid keys found in comma-separated list: %s", input);
391     }
392     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
393   }
394 
395   // Check for direct SSH Ed25519 key format BEFORE checking for file paths
396   // (SSH keys can contain '/' in base64, which would match path_looks_like_path)
397   if (strncmp(input, "ssh-ed25519", 11) == 0) {
398     asciichat_error_t result = parse_public_key(input, &keys_out[0]);
399     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
400       *num_keys = 1;
401     }
402     return result;
403   }
404 
405   // Check if this is a GitHub/GitLab reference - these support multiple keys
406   if (strncmp(input, "github:", 7) == 0 || strncmp(input, "gitlab:", 7) == 0) {
407     const char *username = input + 7; // Skip "github:" or "gitlab:"
408     bool is_github = (strncmp(input, "github:", 7) == 0);
409     bool is_gpg = (strstr(username, ".gpg") != NULL);
410 
411     char **keys = NULL;
412     size_t num_fetched_keys = 0;
413     asciichat_error_t result;
414 
415     if (is_github) {
416       if (is_gpg) {
417         result = fetch_github_gpg_keys(username, &keys, &num_fetched_keys);
418       } else {
419         result = fetch_github_ssh_keys(username, &keys, &num_fetched_keys);
420       }
421     } else {
422       if (is_gpg) {
423         result = fetch_gitlab_gpg_keys(username, &keys, &num_fetched_keys);
424       } else {
425         result = fetch_gitlab_ssh_keys(username, &keys, &num_fetched_keys);
426       }
427     }
428 
429     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || num_fetched_keys == 0) {
430       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to fetch keys from %s for user: %s", is_github ? "GitHub" : "GitLab",
431                        username);
432     }
433 
434     // Parse each fetched key (only Ed25519 keys will succeed)
435     for (size_t i = 0; i < num_fetched_keys && *num_keys < max_keys; i++) {
436       if (parse_public_key(keys[i], &keys_out[*num_keys]) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
437         (*num_keys)++;
438       }
439       // Non-Ed25519 keys are silently skipped
440     }
441 
442     // Free the keys array
443     for (size_t i = 0; i < num_fetched_keys; i++) {
444       SAFE_FREE(keys[i]);
445     }
446     SAFE_FREE(keys);
447 
448     if (*num_keys == 0) {
449       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "No valid Ed25519 keys found for %s user: %s", is_github ? "GitHub" : "GitLab",
450                        username);
451     }
452 
453     log_debug("Parsed %zu Ed25519 key(s) from %s user: %s", *num_keys, is_github ? "GitHub" : "GitLab", username);
454     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
455   }
456 
457   // For file paths, delegate to parse_keys_from_file
458   if (path_looks_like_path(input)) {
459     return parse_keys_from_file(input, keys_out, num_keys, max_keys);
460   }
461 
462   // For all other formats, use single-key parsing
463   asciichat_error_t result = parse_public_key(input, &keys_out[0]);
464   if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
465     *num_keys = 1;
466   }
467   return result;
468 }

    log_debug, log_warn, parse_keys_from_file(),

asciichat_error_t parse_ssh_ed25519_line (const char *
  line, uint8_t ed25519_pk[32])


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/crypto/ssh/ssh_keys.h>


Parse SSH Ed25519 public key from "ssh-ed25519
    AAAAC3..." format.


Parameters


line SSH key line to parse (must not be NULL)


ed25519_pk Output buffer for Ed25519 public key (32 bytes, must not be
  NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Parses SSH Ed25519 public key from standard SSH public key format.
    Format: "ssh-ed25519 <base64_key> [comment]"




Note


Format: Expects "ssh-ed25519" prefix followed
  by base64-encoded public key. Optional comment after key is ignored.



Base64 decoding: Decodes base64 key to extract 32-byte Ed25519
    public key. Validates key format and length.




Key size: Ed25519 public key is exactly 32 bytes. Returns error if
    decoded key is not 32 bytes.




Warning


Key format: Must start with "ssh-ed25519".
  Other key types (RSA, ECDSA) are NOT supported.


Definition at line 163 of file ssh_keys.c.


163                                                                                    {
164   if (!line || !ed25519_pk) {
165     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: line=%p, ed25519_pk=%p", line, ed25519_pk);
166   }
167 
168   // Use regex to parse and extract SSH public key components
169   char *base64_key = NULL, *comment = NULL;
170   if (!crypto_regex_match_public_key(line, &base64_key, &comment)) {
171     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "SSH key line does not match expected format");
172   }
173 
174   // Base64 decode
175   uint8_t *blob;
176   size_t blob_len;
177   if (base64_decode_ssh_key(base64_key, strlen(base64_key), &blob, &blob_len) != 0) {
178     SAFE_FREE(base64_key);
179     SAFE_FREE(comment);
180     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to decode SSH key base64 data");
181   }
182 
183   // Clean up regex-extracted strings
184   SAFE_FREE(base64_key);
185   SAFE_FREE(comment);
186 
187   // Parse SSH key blob structure:
188   // [4 bytes: length of "ssh-ed25519"]
189   // [11 bytes: "ssh-ed25519"]
190   // [4 bytes: length of public key (32)]
191   // [32 bytes: Ed25519 public key]
192 
193   if (blob_len < SSH_KEY_HEADER_SIZE) {
194     SAFE_FREE(blob);
195     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "SSH key blob too small: %zu bytes (expected at least %d)", blob_len,
196                      SSH_KEY_HEADER_SIZE);
197   }
198 
199   // Extract Ed25519 public key (last 32 bytes)
200   memcpy(ed25519_pk, blob + blob_len - ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
201   SAFE_FREE(blob);
202 
203   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
204 }

asciichat_error_t parse_ssh_keys_from_response (const char
  * response_text, size_t response_len, char *** keys_out, size_t * num_keys,
  size_t max_keys)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/crypto/https_keys.h>


Parse SSH keys from HTTPS response text.


Parameters


response_text HTTPS response containing SSH keys
  (must not be NULL)


response_len Length of response text


keys_out Output array of parsed SSH keys (must not be NULL)


num_keys Output parameter for number of keys parsed (must not be NULL)


max_keys Maximum number of keys to parse (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Parses SSH keys from HTTPS response text (one key per line).
    Extracts Ed25519 keys and allocates memory for each key string.




Note


Response format: Expects one SSH key per line. Format:
  "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3... comment" (one key per line)



Key filtering: Only Ed25519 SSH keys are parsed. Other key types
    (RSA, ECDSA) are skipped.




Memory allocation: Allocates memory for each key string. Caller
    must free each string and the array.




Maximum keys: Stops parsing after max_keys keys are found.
    Function returns ASCIICHAT_OK even if more keys exist in response.




Warning


Memory leak: Caller must free returned strings and array.
  Function allocates memory that must be freed.


Definition at line 298 of file https_keys.c.


299                                                                                   {
300   if (!response_text || !keys_out || !num_keys) {
301     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for SSH key parsing");
302     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
303   }
304 
305   *num_keys = 0;
306   *keys_out = NULL;
307 
308   // Count the number of SSH keys in the response
309   size_t key_count = 0;
310   const char *line_start = response_text;
311   const char *line_end;
312 
313   while ((line_end = strchr(line_start, '\n')) != NULL) {
314     size_t line_len = line_end - line_start;
315     if (line_len > 0 && line_start[0] != '\r' && line_start[0] != '\n') {
316       key_count++;
317     }
318     line_start = line_end + 1;
319   }
320 
321   // Handle last line if it doesn't end with newline
322   if (line_start < response_text + response_len) {
323     key_count++;
324   }
325 
326   if (key_count == 0) {
327     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "No SSH keys found in response");
328     return ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY;
329   }
330 
331   if (key_count > max_keys) {
332     key_count = max_keys;
333   }
334 
335   // Allocate array for key strings
336   *keys_out = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(char *) * key_count, char **);
337   if (!*keys_out) {
338     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate SSH keys array");
339   }
340 
341   // Parse each SSH key line
342   line_start = response_text;
343   size_t parsed_keys = 0;
344 
345   while (parsed_keys < key_count && (line_end = strchr(line_start, '\n')) != NULL) {
346     size_t line_len = line_end - line_start;
347 
348     // Skip empty lines
349     if (line_len > 0 && line_start[0] != '\r' && line_start[0] != '\n') {
350       // Allocate space for this key line
351       (*keys_out)[parsed_keys] = SAFE_MALLOC(line_len + 1, char *);
352       if (!(*keys_out)[parsed_keys]) {
353         // Cleanup previously allocated keys
354         for (size_t i = 0; i < parsed_keys; i++) {
355           SAFE_FREE((*keys_out)[i]);
356         }
357         SAFE_FREE(*keys_out);
358         *keys_out = NULL;
359         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate SSH key string");
360       }
361 
362       // Copy the key line
363       memcpy((*keys_out)[parsed_keys], line_start, line_len);
364       (*keys_out)[parsed_keys][line_len] = '\0';
365 
366       parsed_keys++;
367     }
368 
369     line_start = line_end + 1;
370   }
371 
372   // Handle last line if it doesn't end with newline
373   if (parsed_keys < key_count && line_start < response_text + response_len) {
374     size_t line_len = (response_text + response_len) - line_start;
375     if (line_len > 0) {
376       (*keys_out)[parsed_keys] = SAFE_MALLOC(line_len + 1, char *);
377       if (!(*keys_out)[parsed_keys]) {
378         // Cleanup previously allocated keys
379         for (size_t i = 0; i < parsed_keys; i++) {
380           SAFE_FREE((*keys_out)[i]);
381         }
382         SAFE_FREE(*keys_out);
383         *keys_out = NULL;
384         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate SSH key string");
385       }
386       memcpy((*keys_out)[parsed_keys], line_start, line_len);
387       (*keys_out)[parsed_keys][line_len] = '\0';
388       parsed_keys++;
389     }
390   }
391 
392   *num_keys = parsed_keys;
393   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
394 }

asciichat_error_t parse_ssh_private_key (const char *
  key_path, private_key_t * key_out)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/crypto/ssh/ssh_keys.h>


Parse SSH Ed25519 private key from file.


Parameters


key_path Path to SSH private key file (must not be
  NULL)


key_out Output structure for parsed private key (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Parses OpenSSH Ed25519 private key from file. Supports both
    encrypted and unencrypted keys.




Note


Key format: Supports OpenSSH private key format
  (openssh-key-v1). Format: "-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----" ...
  "-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----"



Encrypted keys: If key is encrypted, prompts for password and
    decrypts it natively. Uses bcrypt_pbkdf (from libsodium-bcrypt-pbkdf) +
    BearSSL AES (aes256-ctr/aes256-cbc) for OpenSSH format decryption. No
    external tools (ssh-keygen) required.




Key structure: Parses OpenSSH key structure:


    
  
  • Magic: "openssh-key-v1\0"
    • 
  
  • Ciphername: "none" for unencrypted, cipher name for
    encrypted
    • 
  
  • KDF name and options (for encrypted keys)
    • 
  
  • Number of keys (must be 1)
    • 
  
  • Public key (for verification)
    • 
  
  • Private key blob (decrypted if encrypted)
    • 



Ed25519 extraction: Extracts Ed25519 seed (32 bytes) and public
    key (32 bytes) from private key blob. Stores as 64-byte Ed25519 key (seed +
    public).




File validation: Validates file permissions before parsing. Warns
    if file has overly permissive permissions (world-readable).




Warning


Key format: Only Ed25519 keys are supported. RSA/ECDSA
  keys will return error.



File permissions: Private key files should have restrictive
    permissions (0600). Function warns but does not fail on overly permissive
    permissions.




Password: Encrypted keys require password for native decryption.
    Decryption is done using bcrypt_pbkdf + BearSSL AES (supports aes256-ctr and
    aes256-cbc). May fail if password is incorrect, KDF parameters are
    unsupported, or unsupported encryption cipher.




Definition at line 206 of file ssh_keys.c.


206                                                                                       {
207   if (!key_path || !key_out) {
208     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: key_path=%p, key_out=%p", key_path, key_out);
209   }
210 
211   // First, check if we can get the key from ssh-agent (password-free)
212   // This requires reading the public key from the .pub file
213   char pub_key_path[BUFFER_SIZE_LARGE];
214   safe_snprintf(pub_key_path, sizeof(pub_key_path), "%s.pub", key_path);
215 
216   FILE *pub_f = platform_fopen("file_stream", pub_key_path, "r");
217   if (pub_f) {
218     char pub_line[BUFFER_SIZE_LARGE];
219     if (fgets(pub_line, sizeof(pub_line), pub_f)) {
220       public_key_t pub_key = {0};
221       pub_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
222 
223       if (parse_ssh_ed25519_line(pub_line, pub_key.key) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
224         // Check if this key is in ssh-agent
225         if (ssh_agent_has_key(&pub_key)) {
226           fclose(pub_f);
227           log_dev("Key found in ssh-agent - using cached key (no password required)");
228           // Key is in agent, we can use it
229           key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
230           key_out->use_ssh_agent = true;
231           memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519 + 32, pub_key.key, 32); // Copy public key to second half
232           memcpy(key_out->public_key, pub_key.key, 32);       // Also store in public_key field
233           // Note: We don't have the private key seed, but ssh-agent will sign for us
234           return ASCIICHAT_OK;
235         } else {
236           log_debug("Key not found in ssh-agent - will decrypt from file");
237         }
238       }
239     }
240     fclose(pub_f);
241   }
242 
243   // Validate the SSH key file first
244   asciichat_error_t validation_result = validate_ssh_key_file(key_path);
245   if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
246     return validation_result;
247   }
248 
249   // Read the private key file
250   FILE *f = platform_fopen("file_stream", key_path, "r");
251   if (!f) {
252     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Cannot read private key file: %s", key_path);
253   }
254 
255   // Read the entire file
256   char *file_content = NULL;
257   size_t file_size = 0;
258   char buffer[BUFFER_SIZE_XXLARGE];
259   size_t bytes_read;
260 
261   while ((bytes_read = fread(buffer, 1, sizeof(buffer), f)) > 0) {
262     file_content = SAFE_REALLOC(file_content, file_size + bytes_read + 1, char *);
263     if (!file_content) {
264       (void)fclose(f);
265       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Out of memory reading private key file");
266     }
267     memcpy(file_content + file_size, buffer, bytes_read);
268     file_size += bytes_read;
269   }
270   (void)fclose(f);
271 
272   if (file_size == 0) {
273     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
274     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Private key file is empty: %s", key_path);
275   }
276 
277   file_content[file_size] = '\0';
278 
279   // Use regex to extract base64 data from OpenSSH PEM format
280   char *pem_base64_data = NULL;
281   if (!crypto_regex_extract_pem_base64(file_content, &pem_base64_data)) {
282     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
283     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid OpenSSH private key format: %s", key_path);
284   }
285 
286   // Remove any whitespace/newlines from the base64 data
287   char *clean_base64 = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(pem_base64_data) + 1, char *);
288   char *clean_ptr = clean_base64;
289   for (const char *p = pem_base64_data; *p; p++) {
290     if (*p != '\n' && *p != '\r' && *p != ' ' && *p != '\t') {
291       *clean_ptr++ = *p;
292     }
293   }
294   *clean_ptr = '\0';
295   SAFE_FREE(pem_base64_data);
296 
297   // Decode the base64 data
298   uint8_t *key_blob;
299   size_t key_blob_len;
300   asciichat_error_t decode_result = base64_decode_ssh_key(clean_base64, strlen(clean_base64), &key_blob, &key_blob_len);
301   SAFE_FREE(clean_base64);
302 
303   if (decode_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
304     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
305     return decode_result;
306   }
307 
308   // Parse the OpenSSH private key structure
309   // Format: [4 bytes: magic] [4 bytes: ciphername length] [ciphername] [4 bytes: kdfname length] [kdfname]
310   //         [4 bytes: kdfoptions length] [kdfoptions] [4 bytes: num keys] [4 bytes: pubkey length] [pubkey]
311   //         [4 bytes: privkey length] [privkey]
312 
313   if (key_blob_len < 4) {
314     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
315     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
316     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key blob too small: %s", key_path);
317   }
318 
319   // Check magic number (should be "openssh-key-v1\0")
320   if (memcmp(key_blob, "openssh-key-v1\0", 15) != 0) {
321     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
322     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
323     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid OpenSSH private key magic: %s", key_path);
324   }
325 
326   size_t offset = 15; // Skip magic
327 
328   // Read ciphername
329   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
330     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
331     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
332     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at ciphername: %s", key_path);
333   }
334 
335   uint32_t ciphername_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
336   offset += 4;
337 
338   if (offset + ciphername_len > key_blob_len) {
339     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
340     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
341     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at ciphername data: %s", key_path);
342   }
343 
344   // Store the position of ciphername for later use
345   size_t ciphername_pos = offset;
346 
347   // Check if key is encrypted
348   bool is_encrypted = (ciphername_len > 0 && memcmp(key_blob + offset, "none", 4) != 0);
349 
350   offset += ciphername_len;
351 
352   // Skip kdfname and kdfoptions (we don't support encryption)
353   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
354     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
355     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
356     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at kdfname: %s", key_path);
357   }
358 
359   uint32_t kdfname_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
360   offset += 4;
361 
362   // Store the position of kdfname for later use
363   size_t kdfname_pos = offset;
364 
365   offset += kdfname_len;
366 
367   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
368     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
369     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
370     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at kdfoptions: %s", key_path);
371   }
372 
373   uint32_t kdfoptions_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
374   offset += 4 + kdfoptions_len;
375 
376   // Handle encrypted keys
377   if (is_encrypted) {
378     // Parse the cipher name from the stored position
379     char ciphername[32] = {0};
380     if (ciphername_len > 0 && ciphername_len < sizeof(ciphername)) {
381       memcpy(ciphername, key_blob + ciphername_pos, ciphername_len);
382     }
383 
384     // Parse the KDF name from the stored position
385     char kdfname[32] = {0};
386     if (kdfname_len > 0 && kdfname_len < sizeof(kdfname)) {
387       memcpy(kdfname, key_blob + kdfname_pos, kdfname_len);
388     }
389 
390     log_debug("Cipher: %s, KDF: %s", ciphername, kdfname);
391 
392     // Check if we support this encryption method
393     if (strcmp(ciphername, "aes256-ctr") != 0 && strcmp(ciphername, "aes256-cbc") != 0) {
394       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
395       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
396       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY,
397                        "Unsupported cipher '%s' for encrypted SSH key: %s\n"
398                        "Supported ciphers: aes256-ctr, aes256-cbc",
399                        ciphername, key_path);
400     }
401 
402     if (strcmp(kdfname, "bcrypt") != 0) {
403       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
404       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
405       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY,
406                        "Unsupported KDF '%s' for encrypted SSH key: %s\n"
407                        "Only bcrypt KDF is supported",
408                        kdfname, key_path);
409     }
410 
411     // Parse KDF options (bcrypt salt and rounds)
412     if (kdfoptions_len < 8) {
413       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
414       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
415       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid KDF options length: %s", key_path);
416     }
417 
418     // Parse KDF options: [salt_length:4][salt:N][rounds:4]
419     size_t kdf_opt_offset = offset - kdfoptions_len;
420 
421     // Read salt length
422     if (kdf_opt_offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
423       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
424       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
425       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "KDF options truncated at salt length: %s", key_path);
426     }
427     uint32_t salt_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[kdf_opt_offset]);
428     kdf_opt_offset += 4;
429 
430     // Read salt
431     if (salt_len != 16) {
432       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
433       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
434       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Unexpected bcrypt salt length %u (expected 16): %s", salt_len, key_path);
435     }
436     if (kdf_opt_offset + salt_len > key_blob_len) {
437       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
438       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
439       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "KDF options truncated at salt data: %s", key_path);
440     }
441     uint8_t bcrypt_salt[16];
442     memcpy(bcrypt_salt, key_blob + kdf_opt_offset, salt_len);
443     kdf_opt_offset += salt_len;
444 
445     // Read rounds
446     if (kdf_opt_offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
447       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
448       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
449       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "KDF options truncated at rounds: %s", key_path);
450     }
451     uint32_t bcrypt_rounds = read_u32_be(&key_blob[kdf_opt_offset]);
452 
453     // Check for password in environment variable first
454     const char *env_password = platform_getenv("ASCII_CHAT_KEY_PASSWORD");
455     char *password = NULL;
456     if (env_password && strlen(env_password) > 0) {
457       // Use password from environment variable
458       password = platform_strdup(env_password);
459       if (!password) {
460         SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
461         SAFE_FREE(file_content);
462         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for password");
463       }
464     } else {
465       // Prompt for password interactively - allocate buffer for input
466       password = SAFE_MALLOC(1024, char *);
467       if (!password) {
468         SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
469         SAFE_FREE(file_content);
470         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for password");
471       }
472       if (prompt_password_simple("Encrypted SSH key - enter passphrase", password, 1024) != 0) {
473         SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
474         SAFE_FREE(file_content);
475         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to read passphrase for encrypted key: %s", key_path);
476       }
477     }
478 
479     // Native OpenSSH key decryption using bcrypt_pbkdf + BearSSL AES
480 
481     // Skip past the unencrypted public key section to get to encrypted private keys
482     // Format: [num_keys:4][pubkey_len:4][pubkey:N]...[encrypted_len:4][encrypted:N]
483 
484     // Read num_keys
485     if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
486       sodium_memzero(password, strlen(password));
487       SAFE_FREE(password);
488       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
489       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
490       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Encrypted key truncated at num_keys: %s", key_path);
491     }
492     uint32_t num_keys = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
493     offset += 4;
494     log_debug("num_keys=%u", num_keys);
495 
496     // Skip all public keys
497     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_keys; i++) {
498       if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
499         sodium_memzero(password, strlen(password));
500         SAFE_FREE(password);
501         SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
502         SAFE_FREE(file_content);
503         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Encrypted key truncated at pubkey %u length: %s", i, key_path);
504       }
505       uint32_t pubkey_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
506       offset += 4;
507       log_debug("Skipping public key %u: %u bytes", i, pubkey_len);
508 
509       if (offset + pubkey_len > key_blob_len) {
510         sodium_memzero(password, strlen(password));
511         SAFE_FREE(password);
512         SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
513         SAFE_FREE(file_content);
514         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Encrypted key truncated at pubkey %u data: %s", i, key_path);
515       }
516       offset += pubkey_len;
517     }
518 
519     // Read encrypted private keys length
520     if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
521       sodium_memzero(password, strlen(password));
522       SAFE_FREE(password);
523       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
524       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
525       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Encrypted key truncated at encrypted_len: %s", key_path);
526     }
527     uint32_t encrypted_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
528     offset += 4;
529 
530     // Now offset points to the actual encrypted data
531     size_t encrypted_data_start = offset;
532     size_t encrypted_data_len = encrypted_len;
533 
534     if (encrypted_data_len < 16) {
535       sodium_memzero(password, strlen(password));
536       SAFE_FREE(password);
537       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
538       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
539       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Encrypted data too small: %s", key_path);
540     }
541 
542     // Extract encrypted blob (includes everything from offset onwards)
543     const uint8_t *encrypted_blob = key_blob + encrypted_data_start;
544 
545     // Call native decryption function
546     uint8_t *decrypted_blob = NULL;
547     size_t decrypted_blob_len = 0;
548 
549     // Note: decrypt_openssh_private_key derives IV from bcrypt_pbkdf, not from encrypted data
550     asciichat_error_t decrypt_result =
551         decrypt_openssh_private_key(encrypted_blob, encrypted_data_len, password, bcrypt_salt, salt_len, bcrypt_rounds,
552                                     ciphername, &decrypted_blob, &decrypted_blob_len);
553 
554     // Clean up password immediately after use
555     sodium_memzero(password, strlen(password));
556     SAFE_FREE(password);
557 
558     if (decrypt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
559       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
560       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
561       return decrypt_result;
562     }
563 
564     // Parse the decrypted private key structure
565     // OpenSSH format (decrypted):
566     //   [checkint1:4][checkint2:4][keytype:string][pubkey:string][privkey:string][comment:string][padding:N]
567 
568     if (decrypted_blob_len < 8) {
569       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
570       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
571       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
572       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Decrypted data too small (no checkints): %s", key_path);
573     }
574 
575     // Verify checkints (first 8 bytes should be two identical 32-bit values)
576     uint32_t checkint1 = read_u32_be(&decrypted_blob[0]);
577     uint32_t checkint2 = read_u32_be(&decrypted_blob[4]);
578     if (checkint1 != checkint2) {
579       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
580       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
581       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
582       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY,
583                        "Incorrect passphrase or corrupted key (checkint mismatch): %s\n"
584                        "Expected matching checkints, got 0x%08x != 0x%08x",
585                        key_path, checkint1, checkint2);
586     }
587 
588     // Parse the decrypted private key structure manually
589     // Format after checkints: [keytype:string][pubkey:string][privkey:string][comment:string][padding:N]
590     size_t dec_offset = 8; // Skip checkints
591 
592     // Read keytype length
593     if (dec_offset + 4 > decrypted_blob_len) {
594       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
595       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
596       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
597       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Decrypted key truncated at keytype length: %s", key_path);
598     }
599     uint32_t keytype_len = read_u32_be(&decrypted_blob[dec_offset]);
600     dec_offset += 4;
601 
602     // Read keytype
603     if (dec_offset + keytype_len > decrypted_blob_len) {
604       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
605       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
606       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
607       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Decrypted key truncated at keytype data: %s", key_path);
608     }
609     char keytype[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL / 4] = {0}; // SSH key type strings are short
610     if (keytype_len > 0 && keytype_len < sizeof(keytype)) {
611       memcpy(keytype, decrypted_blob + dec_offset, keytype_len);
612     }
613     dec_offset += keytype_len;
614 
615     // Check if it's Ed25519
616     if (strcmp(keytype, "ssh-ed25519") != 0) {
617       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
618       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
619       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
620       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Unsupported key type after decryption: '%s'", keytype);
621     }
622 
623     // Read public key length
624     if (dec_offset + 4 > decrypted_blob_len) {
625       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
626       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
627       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
628       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Decrypted key truncated at pubkey length: %s", key_path);
629     }
630     uint32_t pubkey_data_len = read_u32_be(&decrypted_blob[dec_offset]);
631     dec_offset += 4;
632 
633     // Read public key (32 bytes for Ed25519)
634     if (pubkey_data_len != 32) {
635       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
636       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
637       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
638       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid Ed25519 public key length: %u (expected 32)", pubkey_data_len);
639     }
640     if (dec_offset + pubkey_data_len > decrypted_blob_len) {
641       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
642       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
643       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
644       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Decrypted key truncated at pubkey data: %s", key_path);
645     }
646     uint8_t ed25519_pk[32];
647     memcpy(ed25519_pk, decrypted_blob + dec_offset, 32);
648     dec_offset += 32;
649 
650     // Read private key length
651     if (dec_offset + 4 > decrypted_blob_len) {
652       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
653       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
654       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
655       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Decrypted key truncated at privkey length: %s", key_path);
656     }
657     uint32_t privkey_data_len = read_u32_be(&decrypted_blob[dec_offset]);
658     dec_offset += 4;
659 
660     // Read private key (64 bytes for Ed25519: 32-byte seed + 32-byte public key)
661     if (privkey_data_len != 64) {
662       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
663       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
664       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
665       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Invalid Ed25519 private key length: %u (expected 64)", privkey_data_len);
666     }
667     if (dec_offset + privkey_data_len > decrypted_blob_len) {
668       SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
669       SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
670       SAFE_FREE(file_content);
671       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Decrypted key truncated at privkey data: %s", key_path);
672     }
673     uint8_t ed25519_sk[64];
674     memcpy(ed25519_sk, decrypted_blob + dec_offset, 64);
675     dec_offset += 64;
676 
677     // Populate key_out (ed25519_sk contains: 32-byte seed + 32-byte public key)
678     key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
679     memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519, ed25519_sk, 32);           // Seed (first 32 bytes)
680     memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519 + 32, ed25519_sk + 32, 32); // Public key (next 32 bytes)
681 
682     // Clean up decrypted data (sensitive!)
683     sodium_memzero(decrypted_blob, decrypted_blob_len);
684     SAFE_FREE(decrypted_blob);
685     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
686     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
687 
688     log_debug("Successfully parsed decrypted Ed25519 key");
689 
690     // Attempt to add the decrypted key to ssh-agent for future password-free use
691     log_debug("Attempting to add decrypted key to ssh-agent");
692     asciichat_error_t agent_result = ssh_agent_add_key(key_out, key_path);
693     if (agent_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
694       log_debug("Successfully added key to ssh-agent - password will not be required on next run");
695     } else {
696       // Non-fatal: key is already decrypted and loaded, just won't be cached in agent
697       log_warn("Failed to add key to ssh-agent (non-fatal): %s", asciichat_error_string(agent_result));
698       log_warn("You can manually add it with: ssh-add %s", key_path);
699     }
700 
701     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
702   }
703 
704   // Read number of keys
705   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
706     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
707     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
708     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at num keys: %s", key_path);
709   }
710 
711   uint32_t num_keys = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
712   offset += 4;
713 
714   log_debug("num_keys=%u, offset=%zu, key_blob_len=%zu", num_keys, offset, key_blob_len);
715   log_debug("Raw bytes at offset %zu: %02x %02x %02x %02x", offset, key_blob[offset], key_blob[offset + 1],
716             key_blob[offset + 2], key_blob[offset + 3]);
717   log_debug("After num_keys, offset=%zu", offset);
718 
719   if (num_keys != 1) {
720     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
721     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
722     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key contains %u keys (expected 1): %s", num_keys, key_path);
723   }
724 
725   // Read public key
726   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
727     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
728     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
729     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at pubkey length: %s", key_path);
730   }
731 
732   log_debug("About to read pubkey_len at offset=%zu, bytes: %02x %02x %02x %02x", offset, key_blob[offset],
733             key_blob[offset + 1], key_blob[offset + 2], key_blob[offset + 3]);
734 
735   uint32_t pubkey_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
736   offset += 4;
737 
738   log_debug("pubkey_len=%u, offset=%zu", pubkey_len, offset);
739 
740   if (offset + pubkey_len > key_blob_len) {
741     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
742     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
743     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at pubkey data: %s", key_path);
744   }
745 
746   // Parse the public key to extract the Ed25519 public key for validation
747   if (pubkey_len < 4) {
748     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
749     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
750     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH public key too small: %s", key_path);
751   }
752 
753   uint32_t key_type_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
754   offset += 4;
755 
756   // Check if it's an Ed25519 key
757   if (key_type_len != 11 || memcmp(key_blob + offset, "ssh-ed25519", 11) != 0) {
758     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
759     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
760     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key is not Ed25519: %s", key_path);
761   }
762 
763   offset += key_type_len; // Skip the key type string
764   log_debug("After skipping key type, offset=%zu", offset);
765 
766   // Read the public key length
767   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
768     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
769     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
770     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at public key length: %s", key_path);
771   }
772 
773   uint32_t pubkey_data_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
774   offset += 4;
775   log_debug("Public key data length: %u, offset=%zu", pubkey_data_len, offset);
776 
777   log_debug("Public key data length: %u (expected 32 for Ed25519)", pubkey_data_len);
778 
779   // For Ed25519, the public key should be 32 bytes, but let's be more flexible
780   if (pubkey_data_len < 32) {
781     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
782     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
783     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH public key data too small (%u bytes, expected at least 32): %s",
784                      pubkey_data_len, key_path);
785   }
786 
787   // Read Ed25519 public key (first 32 bytes) for validation
788   if (offset + 32 > key_blob_len) {
789     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
790     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
791     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at public key: %s", key_path);
792   }
793 
794   uint8_t ed25519_pubkey[32];
795   memcpy(ed25519_pubkey, key_blob + offset, 32);
796   offset += pubkey_data_len; // Skip the entire public key data
797 
798   // Skip the rest of the public key data to get to the private key
799   // We've already parsed: 4 (key_type_len) + 11 (ssh-ed25519) + 4 (pubkey_data_len) + 32 (key) = 51 bytes
800   // So we need to skip the remaining pubkey_len - 51 bytes
801   // Check for underflow before subtraction
802   if (pubkey_len >= 51) {
803     size_t remaining_pubkey = pubkey_len - 51;
804     if (remaining_pubkey > 0) {
805       offset += remaining_pubkey;
806     }
807   }
808 
809   // Read private key
810   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
811     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
812     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
813     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at privkey length: %s", key_path);
814   }
815 
816   uint32_t privkey_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
817   offset += 4;
818 
819   if (offset + privkey_len > key_blob_len) {
820     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
821     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
822     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at privkey data: %s", key_path);
823   }
824 
825   // Parse the private key structure
826   // Format: [4 bytes: checkint1] [4 bytes: checkint2] [4 bytes: key type length] [key type]
827   //         [4 bytes: public key length] [public key] [4 bytes: private key length] [private key]
828   //         [4 bytes: comment length] [comment]
829 
830   if (privkey_len < 8) {
831     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
832     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
833     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key data too small: %s", key_path);
834   }
835 
836   // Verify checkints (should be equal)
837   uint32_t checkint1 = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
838   uint32_t checkint2 = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset + 4]);
839   offset += 8;
840 
841   if (checkint1 != checkint2) {
842     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
843     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
844     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key checkints don't match: %s", key_path);
845   }
846 
847   // Skip key type
848   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
849     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
850     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
851     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at key type length: %s", key_path);
852   }
853 
854   uint32_t key_type_len_priv = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
855   // Check for integer overflow before adding to offset
856   if (key_type_len_priv > key_blob_len || offset + 4 + key_type_len_priv > key_blob_len) {
857     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
858     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
859     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key type length overflow: %u", key_type_len_priv);
860   }
861   offset += 4 + key_type_len_priv;
862 
863   // Skip public key
864   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
865     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
866     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
867     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at pubkey length: %s", key_path);
868   }
869 
870   uint32_t pubkey_len_priv = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
871   // Check for integer overflow before adding to offset
872   if (pubkey_len_priv > key_blob_len || offset + 4 + pubkey_len_priv > key_blob_len) {
873     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
874     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
875     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key public key length overflow: %u", pubkey_len_priv);
876   }
877   offset += 4 + pubkey_len_priv;
878 
879   // Read private key
880   if (offset + 4 > key_blob_len) {
881     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
882     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
883     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at privkey length: %s", key_path);
884   }
885 
886   uint32_t privkey_data_len = read_u32_be(&key_blob[offset]);
887   offset += 4;
888 
889   if (offset + privkey_data_len > key_blob_len) {
890     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
891     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
892     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key truncated at privkey data: %s", key_path);
893   }
894 
895   // The private key data should be at least 64 bytes (32 bytes private key + 32 bytes public key)
896   // But OpenSSH format may have additional data
897   if (privkey_data_len < 64) {
898     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
899     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
900     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key data length is %u (expected at least 64): %s",
901                      privkey_data_len, key_path);
902   }
903 
904   // Extract the Ed25519 private key (first 32 bytes)
905   uint8_t ed25519_privkey[32];
906   memcpy(ed25519_privkey, key_blob + offset, 32);
907 
908   // Verify the public key matches
909   // The public key in the privkey section is raw Ed25519, while the one in pubkey section is SSH format
910   // We need to compare the raw public key from privkey with the raw public key extracted from pubkey
911   // Use constant-time comparison for cryptographic material
912   if (sodium_memcmp(key_blob + offset + 32, ed25519_pubkey, 32) != 0) {
913     SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
914     SAFE_FREE(file_content);
915     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "OpenSSH private key public key mismatch: %s", key_path);
916   }
917 
918   // Initialize the private key structure
919   memset(key_out, 0, sizeof(private_key_t));
920   key_out->type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
921 
922   // Store the actual private key (seed + public key = 64 bytes)
923   // Ed25519 private key format: [32 bytes seed][32 bytes public key]
924   memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519, ed25519_privkey, 32);     // Store the seed (first 32 bytes)
925   memcpy(key_out->key.ed25519 + 32, ed25519_pubkey, 32); // Store the public key (next 32 bytes)
926 
927   // Also store the public key in the public_key field for easy access
928   memcpy(key_out->public_key, ed25519_pubkey, 32);
929 
930   // Set a comment
931   SAFE_STRNCPY(key_out->key_comment, "ssh-ed25519", sizeof(key_out->key_comment) - 1);
932 
933   SAFE_FREE(key_blob);
934   SAFE_FREE(file_content);
935 
936   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
937 }

    BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL, BUFFER_SIZE_XXLARGE,
    crypto_regex_extract_pem_base64(), private_key_t::ed25519,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    private_key_t::key, public_key_t::key,
    private_key_t::key_comment, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,

asciichat_error_t private_key_to_x25519 (const
  private_key_t * key, uint8_t x25519_sk[32])


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/crypto/keys.h>


Convert private key to X25519 for Diffie-Hellman key exchange.


Parameters


key Private key to convert (must not be NULL)


x25519_sk Output buffer for X25519 private key (32 bytes)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Converts private key to X25519 format for key exchange:


    
  
  • Ed25519 → X25519: Uses libsodium conversion function
      (crypto_sign_ed25519_sk_to_curve25519)
    • 
  
  • X25519: Passthrough (key is already in X25519 format)
    • 



Note


Conversion: Ed25519 keys are converted using libsodium's
  conversion function. Conversion is mathematically safe (same curve, different
  representation).



Ed25519 keys: Extracts 32-byte seed from Ed25519 key (first 32
    bytes of ed25519 union). Converts seed to X25519 scalar using libsodium.




X25519 passthrough: If key is already X25519, key is copied
    directly.




Warning


All keys must be 32 bytes. Function validates key size
  before conversion.


Definition at line 493 of file keys.c.


493                                                                                          {
494   if (!key || !x25519_sk) {
495     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for private_key_to_x25519");
496   }
497 
498   if (key->type == KEY_TYPE_X25519) {
499     // Passthrough for X25519 keys
500     memcpy(x25519_sk, key->key.x25519, 32);
501     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
502   }
503 
504   if (key->type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
505     // Convert Ed25519 to X25519 (Ed25519 private key is 64 bytes: seed + public)
506     return ed25519_to_x25519_private(key->key.ed25519, x25519_sk);
507   }
508 
509   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Unsupported key type for X25519 conversion: %d", key->type);
510 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, private_key_t::ed25519,
    ed25519_to_x25519_private(), ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, private_key_t::key,
    KEY_TYPE_ED25519, KEY_TYPE_X25519, SET_ERRNO,
    private_key_t::type, and private_key_t::x25519.






asciichat_error_t public_key_to_x25519 (const
  public_key_t * key, uint8_t x25519_pk[32])


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/crypto/keys.h>


Convert public key to X25519 for Diffie-Hellman key exchange.


Parameters


key Public key to convert (must not be NULL)


x25519_pk Output buffer for X25519 public key (32 bytes)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Converts public key to X25519 format for key exchange:


    
  
  • Ed25519 → X25519: Uses libsodium conversion function
      (crypto_sign_ed25519_pk_to_curve25519)
    • 
  
  • X25519: Passthrough (key is already in X25519 format)
    • 
  
  • GPG: Already derived to X25519 during GPG parsing
    • 



Note


Conversion: Ed25519 keys are converted using libsodium's
  conversion function. Conversion is mathematically safe (same curve, different
  representation).



X25519 passthrough: If key is already X25519, key is copied
    directly.




GPG keys: GPG keys are already converted to X25519 during parsing.
    No additional conversion needed.




Warning


All keys must be 32 bytes. Function validates key size
  before conversion.

474                                                                                        {
475   if (!key || !x25519_pk) {
476     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for public_key_to_x25519");
477   }
478 
479   if (key->type == KEY_TYPE_X25519) {
480     // Passthrough for X25519 keys
481     memcpy(x25519_pk, key->key, 32);
482     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
483   }
484 
485   if (key->type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
486     // Convert Ed25519 to X25519
487     return ed25519_to_x25519_public(key->key, x25519_pk);
488   }
489 
490   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Unsupported key type for X25519 conversion: %d", key->type);

  gpg_key_text)
